topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Reforms
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments and reforms to the Medicaid and Medicare programs under the Social Security Act.

Document 1: This bill amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to include as an activity under the Medicare Integrity Program an annual audit of payment claims under a state Medicaid plan.
to determine if any payments for family planning services and supplies violated federal law that restricts the use of funds under Medicaid for abortions.

Document 3: Medicare Drug Savings Act of 2011 - Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to require drug manufacturers to pay the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) drug rebates for rebate eligible (low-income) individuals.

Document 5: Territorial Health Parity Act of 2009 - Amends title XI of the Social Security Act (SSA) to sunset at the end of FY2009 the limitation on Medicaid payments to Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, the Northern Mariana Islands, and American Samoa.
Document 6: Personalize Your Care Act of 2013 - Amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to cover a Voluntary Advance Care Planning Consultation.
Document 7: Medicare and Medicaid Improvements and Adjustments Act of 2015 - This bill amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to modify payment rules and other provisions related to the Medicare and Medicaid programs.
Document 9: Patient and Pharmacy Protection Act of 2007 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act, as amended by the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans' Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act, 2007, to limit to Controlled Substances Act schedule II narcotics the use of tamper-resistant prescription pads required under the Medicaid program for payment of amounts expended for covered outpatient drugs.
Document 10: Helping Ensure Life- and Limb-Saving Access to Podiatric Physicians Act or HELLPP Act - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to include podiatrists as physicians in order to cover their services under the Medicaid program.

topic.name: Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving transparency and oversight of surveillance activities conducted under the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA).

Document 1: Requires the Department of Justice (DOJ) to publicly disclose any decision of a court established by the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) that surveillance activities conducted by the U.S. government have violated a particular U.S. law or constitutional provision.
Requires the Director of National Intelligence (DNI) to provide information on surveillance activities to Congress.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to authorize the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to apply for an order requiring the production of tangible things pursuant to an investigation to obtain foreign intelligence information not concerning a U.S. person.
Requires the Attorney General to establish minimization and destruction procedures.
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to impose limits on roving electronic surveillance by revising specifications and adding directions required to be included in the order of a designated judge approving such surveillance.
Directs the Attorney General (AG) to establish minimization and destruction procedures.
Document 5: Government Surveillance Transparency Act of 2013 - Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to authorize an entity that is required to comply with an order or directive issued by a FISA court to make publicly available every 90 days with respect to the preceding 90-day period.
The aggregate number of orders or directives with which such entity was required to comply.
Document 10: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to include the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board as a recipient of FISA court procedural rule changes, decisions, and pleadings.

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Access
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating mortgage lending practices, ensuring access to mortgage finance, and providing protections for homeowners.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to the permission that a creditor may presume that a residential mortgage loan has met the requirement that, at the time the loan is consummated, the consumer has a reasonable ability to repay it.
Treats as a qualified mortgage any residential mortgage loan made by a creditor so long as it appears on the creditor's balance sheet.
Document 2: Extends the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) demonstration projects on the guaranteeing of veterans' adjustable rate and hybrid adjustable rate mortgage loans.
Similar to which the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development insures such mortgages under the National Housing Act.
Document 3: Appoints the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) receiver of the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac).
Directs the FHFA to commence liquidation of the GSEs immediately upon their placement into receivership.
Document 4: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 (RESPA) to require the mortgagee, upon default of a federally related mortgage loan secured by a lien on the borrower's principal residence, to engage in loss mitigation activities.
Prohibits initiation of foreclosure on any mortgage loan if the mortgagee or servicer has at any time failed to comply with specified loss mitigation requirements.
Document 5: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 to require a servicer of a federally related or federally backed mortgage loan to provide specified notice to the borrower and applicable taxing district.
Unless specified requirements are met, a servicer of a loan insured by the Federal Housing Administration may not abandon an initiated foreclosure.
Document 6: Amends the National Housing Act to direct the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to establish and collect additional annual premium payments for insured mortgages.
Increases the 30-year annual premium for an insured mortgage whose original principal obligation exceeds 95% of the remaining principal balance.
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to deny any construction of the prohibition against residential mortgage loan originator steering incentives as prohibiting a creditor or mortgage originator from reducing compensation to an employee mortgage originator.
Allows a subsequent offer to the consumer that consists only of a reduction in the APR.
Document 8: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require the servicer of a federally related mortgage to notify each holder of a subordinate lien on the property securing the loan of a request for a short sale.
Requires a subordinate lien holder to respond in writing to the servicer within 45 days after receiving the notification.
Document 9: Amends the National Housing Act to revise the eligibility requirements for mortgages insured by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to include a mortgage executed by a health center.
Document 10: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to create a safe harbor from lawsuit for creditors that are insured depository institutions for any failure to comply with certain requirements with respect to a residential mortgage loan.
A safe harbor from lawsuit is also created for mortgage originators for steering a consumer to a residential mortgage loan.

topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at regulating and overseeing financial institutions to ensure stability and prevent systemic risks in the banking sector.

Document 4: This bill amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision.
The FSOC must make these final determinations using an indicator-based measurement approach established by the Basel Committee.
Document 5: Directs the Comptroller General to study any bank holding company having more than $500 billion in consolidated assets with respect to favorable pricing of its debt relative to its risk profile.
Defines 'economic benefit' as the difference between actual loan terms offered, debt or equity prices, or asset values and a reasonable estimate of what such terms, prices, or values might have been.
Document 6: Cites circumstances under which, for purposes of determining capital requirements, such an institution may treat a non-accrual loan as an accrual loan.
Prohibits the appropriate federal banking agency from imposing any additional accounting requirements upon an insured depository institution.
Document 9: Directs the Inspector General (IG) of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) to study the impact of the failure of insured depository institutions.
Requires the study to examine the impact of significant losses arising from loans for which all payments were current on the ability of insured depository institutions to raise additional capital.
